calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Continued To May-10-27 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-02-2025 Continued To Oct-05-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas
For Production Of Business Records; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Counsel In Support Of The Motion To Quash
Off Calendar Nov-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BILLY BOOKER's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records.

Plaintiff's motion to quash eight deposition subpoenas (Kaiser Permanente; Kaiser Permanente Central Support Services; Kaiser Permanente Radiology; California Department of Healthcare Services - Medi-Cal Assistance Program; The Rawlings Company LLC; Maurice K. Fretty Chiropractic, Inc.; Boomerang Healthcare; American Medical Response Ambulance Service, Inc.) is DENIED.

Plaintiff's motion pertains to the subpoenas served on 12/9/24. (Christiansen Decl., Ex. B.) The parties subsequently met and conferred and defendant issued amended subpoenas (with no objection) and entered into a first look agreement regarding the Kaiser records. (Le Decl., pars. 10-16, Exs. 10, 11.) This motion is untimely, improper and/or plaintiff has forfeited any objections as to the amended subpoenas.

Defendant established that it is entitled to discover medical records regarding plaintiff's hip and lower back from 1/1/80 until the present because he admitted he had "[p]re-existing hip and lower back problems" "[a]pproximately in the 1980s and 1990s." (Le Decl., Ex. 2 at pp. 9:24-27, 14:19-15:1; Johnson v. Matson Nav. Co. (1958) 163 Cal.App.2d 336, 338 [holding "evidence of the former injury was admissible to show that appellant's present condition resulted in whole or in part from that injury."].) "[P]laintiffs . . . may not withhold information which relates to any physical or mental condition which they have put in issue by bringing th[e] lawsuit." (Britt v. Superior Ct. (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 864.)

The court orders the parties to comply with their first look agreement as to the Kaiser Records and that the records identified in the amended subpoenas (which are limited to the injuries in this case-hip, left eye, head, left arm left shoulder, and back) be produced.

Plaintiff Billy Booker did not demonstrate he acted with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Plaintiff Billy Booker shall pay $4,200 to Defendant City and County of San Francsico as sanctions, payment no later than January 9, 2026. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.030, 2025.420(h).)

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
